## Free to Use and Reuse: Independence Day

American Independence Day, also known as the Fourth of July, became a federal holiday in 1938. The pictures highlight events and people from the American Revolution, the Declaration of Independence, lively celebrations, and red-white-and-blue symbols.

Browse more content that is free to use and reuse

* Color photo of red and white fireworks erupting in the sky over the Lincoln Memorial, the Washington Monument, and the U.S. Capitol Building in the background. The fireworks are reflecting on the river in the foreground, and there are boats on the water. July 4th fireworks, Washington, D.C. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, 2008. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of Black people, primarily women, gathering near a rural store. An American flag hangs from a line running between the two-story building and a large tree. A Fourth of July celebration, St. Helena Island, S.C. Photo by Marion Post Wolcott, 1939. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Page with cursive writing in brown ink. Thomas Jefferson. Original draft of the Declaration of Independence, 1776. Manuscript Division](https://www.loc.gov/exhibits/jefferson/images/vc49.jpg)
* Black-and-white photo of two white children on a parade float covered in stars and stripes bunting. A girl is sitting on the front, and a boy is waving a small flag as he stands on top of the float. Float in the Fourth of July parade at Vale, Oregon. Photo by Russell Lee, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows an older white man with a beard wearing red and white striped pants and a dark coat with white stars. He holds a gun with a bayonet attached as he runs past exploding shells. Uncle Sam's birthday, July 4th, 1776-1918: 142 years young and going strong! Poster, 1918. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of a smiling child wearing a hat and holding an armful of fireworks with an American flag. The Fourth of July: The Day Before. Photo Universal View Co, 1906. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Color photo of a large, green statue depicting a woman wearing a crown and a draped dress on top of a pedestal. Her left arm holds a tablet, and her right hand is holding a torch up in the air. A body of water and a city skyline is in the background. General view of statue looking Northwest. Statue of Liberty, Liberty Island, New York. Photo by Jack Boucher, 1984. Prints & Photographs Division](https://www.loc.gov/resource/hhh.ny1251.photos/?sp=2)
* [Artwork shows the inside of the statue indicating nine floor levels, the distance between levels, and structural supports. Section Looking South - Statue of Liberty, Liberty Island, New York. Drawing by Craig Strong, 1995. Prints & Photographs Division](https://www.loc.gov/resource/hhh.ny1251.sheet/?sp=5)
* Brown-and-purple artwork shows the head and shoulders of an Indigenous American man in front of the hand written Declaration of Independence text. Declaration of Independence. Poster by B. Martin Pedersen and S.D. Scott Printing Co., 1970-1980. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of seven girls and boys using their hands to sing with the American flag hanging behind them. Children performing the Star Spangled Banner in sign language. (Photo was published in 1918 as "Deaf & dumb children of St. Rita's School, Cincinnati, singing Star Spangled Banner). Photo by J.R. Schmidt, 1918. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of a neon sign in the shape of a person holding a firework advertising cheap prices. Dynamite Fireworks sign, Route 17, Myrtle Beach, South Carolina. Photo by John Margolies, 1988. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of golden fireworks erupting from a barge in a body of water with a city skyline in the background. Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania's, renowned Independence Day (July 4) fireworks display. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, 2019. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of Chinese American people carrying a long dragon dance puppet down a street. China in N.Y., 4th of July Parade. Photo by Bain News Service, 1910-1915. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of two white girls wearing white dresses in front of a ticket booth with a younger man standing behind them. One of the girls is reaching her hand up to grab the tickets. Little girls getting tickets for the merry-go-round at the carnival on the Fourth of July. Vale, Oregon. Photo by Russell Lee, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of a white girl and middle-aged woman dancing on a small outdoor stage with musicians playing behind them. Fourth of July Celebration at Stanley Heirs Park on Kayford Mountain, West Virginia. Photo by Terry Eiler, 1996. American Folklife Center
* Color artwork shows eight soldiers wearing red coats firing into a crowd of civilians, several of whom are bleeding on the ground. A dog is in the foreground and buildings line the street. The Bloody Massacre perpetrated in King Street Boston on March 5th 1770, by a party of the 29th Regt. Engraving by Paul Revere, 1770. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows a man as the large central figure with his arms raised and a fearful expression on his face. Behind him, men in hold up knives and clubs. In the foreground bayonets point at the men. "Crispus Attucks," by Herschel Levit, mural at the Recorder of Deeds building, Washington, D.C. Photoh by Carol M. Highsmith, 2010. Prints & Photographs Division
* [On the left, black-and-white artwork shows the profile of a young Black woman sitting at a desk writing on a piece of paper with a quill. On the right, the title page indicates the title, author, and publishing information. Phillis Wheatley, portrait frontispiece and title page for _Poems on Various Subjects_ . Engraving attributed to Scipio Moorhead, 1773. Rare Book Division](https://www.loc.gov/exhibits/treasures/images/tlc0395.jpg)
* Black-and-white artwork depicting many white men in formal attire sitting in a large room. The central figures are a small group of men at a cloth-covered table with papers and a quill pen on it. Declaration of Independence, July 4th, 1776. Engraving by Waterman L. Ormsby, 1876, after painting by John Trumbull, 1818. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork with a profile head-and-shoulders portrait of an older white man with white hair and a laurel crown on his head. Thomas Jefferson, a philosopher, a patriote, and a friend. Aquatint by Mł. Sokolnicki, 1800-1816, after a painting by Tadeusz Kościuszko, ca. 1799. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Black-and-white artwork depicting an army of soldiers holding white flags and their guns over their shoulders. An officer in the foreground extends his sword to an officer of the winning army. [Surrender of Cornwallis at Yorktown]. Wash drawing in an extra-illustrated copy of the book _Recueil d'estampes ... de la Guerre qui a procuré l'indépendance aux Etats Unis de l'Amérique_ . Paris, Ponce et Godefroy [1784?]. Rare Book & Special Collections Division](/resource/cph.3a39934/)
* Artwork shows a head-and-shoulders portrait of an older white man wearing a military uniform and looking directly at the viewer. His Excel: G: Washington Esq: L.L.D. Late commander in chief of the armies of the U.S. of America & president of the Convention of 1787. Mezzotint by Charles Willson Peale,1787. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows an eagle with its wings spread looking up at rays of light while holding two American flags and a basket with two men. The shackles are falling away from the enslaved Black man, and the other man says "Let the oppressed go free." A crowd of people look up at the basket. Men are waving hats and women are waving handkerchiefs. The American Declaration of Independence illustrated. Dominique C. Fabronius, artist. Lithograph by L. Prang & Co.,1861. Prints & Photographs Division
* Artwork surrounds the hand-written text and signatures. At the top and down the sides of the page are bust portraits of white men, a seal with an eagle and a pyramid, and a small picture of signing the Declaration. Official facsimile of the Declaration of Independence. Boston, 1903. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork depicting a large group of naval ships firing cannons at a fort on the harbor flying an American flag. A view of the bombardment of Fort McHenry, near Baltimore, by the British fleet, ... Engraving by John Bower, approximately 1819; re-engraved approx. 1905. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows a young white woman wearing a red and blue dress and a crown with a veil attached to the back. In one hand, she holds an American flag on a thin pole and grasps the end of the flag in her other hand. The Star Spangled Banner. Lithograph by Currier & Ives, 1860-1870. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Color artwork of a large American flag blowing in the wind with blue sky and white clouds behind it. The Star-Spangled Banner. Words by Francis Scott Key. Song composed by John Stafford. Sheet music published by Oliver Ditson, 1918. Music Division](https://www.loc.gov/resource/ihas.100010134.0?st=gallery)
* Black-and-white artwork of Black people of all ages talking, smiling, and eating together in an outdoor festival setting. The Fourth of July in a southern town. Engraving by W.L.S. from a sketch by Horace Bradley, for Harper's Weekly,1888 July 7. Prints & Photographs Division
* Two side-by-side sepia photos of four men with climbing gear eating and drinking on a snowy mountain. Below them are rolling hills and clouds. A Fourth of July banquet 2,000 feet above the clouds, Mount Hood, Oregon. Stereograph by the Keystone View Co., 1904. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork of a tall older white man in red and white striped pants, a blue coat, and a white top hat hugging a short older white man in a red coat and a black top hat. There is a green border with red firecrackers. July the 4th, 1902. Cover of _Puck_ by L. M. Glackens, 1902. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Black-and-white photo of three white adolescent boys and three girls standing around a fireworks stand. [Fourth of July roadside stand near Milwaukee, Wisconsin]. Photo by John Vachon, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division](/resource/fsa.8a33024/)
* Black-and-white, birds-eye-view photo of people standing and positioned to form the shape of a large bell with a crack down the side and the word Liberty at the top. The Human Liberty Bell: 25000 officers and men at Camp Dix, New Jersey. Photo by Mole & Thomas, 1918. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of red and white fireworks erupting in a dark sky over a large, building lit up with red and blue lights. A crowd of people in front of the building are watching the fireworks. Fourth of July, or American Independence Day, fireworks light up the sky over Denver, Colorado. Photo by Carol Highsmith, 2016. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of seven white boys carrying American flags while walking down a city street. People of all ages line each side of the street to watch. Fourth of July parade, Watertown, Wisconsin. Photo by John Vachon, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Color artwork of a white man wearing a white wig and a military uniform standing next to a black horse. Fourth of July Gift. Patriotic song and chorus by Horatio G. King, entitled George Washington. Poster advertisement for the _Family Story Paper_ , [1873-1880?]. Prints & Photographs Division](/resource/cph.3b48195/)
* Black-and-white photo of two white women representing Columbia and the Statue of Liberty posed outdoors with people and cars in the background. Columbia is wearing a long, white dress and holding a flagpole with an American flag, and Lady Liberty is holding a torch and wearing a draped dress and a crown. Fourth of July. Tableau on Ellipse: 'Liberty,' 'Columbia,' and dancers. Photo by Harris & Ewing, 1919. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white artwork depicting a woman covering her ears as two boys beside her play the drums and blow on a bugle. A miniature cannon is firing in the foreground. Fourth of July. Illustration in _Godey's Lady's Book_ , 1864. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of a dark sky and a city skyline with golden fireworks erupting from the tops of skyscrapers. Fireworks over Houston, Texas. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, between 1980 and 2006. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white portrait photo of a group of adolescent white girls wearing identical dresses with striped skirts and stars around the waist. Their tall hats have bows on the top. They look at the camera but only a few are smiling. Takoma Park, July 4th celebration. Photo by the National Photo Co, 1922. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of people of all ages swimming in a body of water with a diving board built on a platform that has an American flag. Bridgeton, New Jersey. Fourth of July picnic. Photo by John Collier, Jr., 1942. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork depicting a Black man and a white man, two women, and a girl raising the American flag on a tall flagpole. In the background, the sun is rising behind a large building with a dome. The flag that has waved one hundred years--A scene on the morning of the fourth day of July 1876. Dominique C. Fabronius, artist. Chromolithograph by National Chromo Co., 1876. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of a white boy looking at boxes of cap pistols through a shop window. Fireworks for sale, Fourth of July, Vale, Oregon. Photo by Russell Lee, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork of a young white woman wearing a bathing costume made of a red and white striped skirt and a blue top with white stars. She is wearing a red bathing cap and standing on a beach shore. Follow the flag. Cover for _Puck_ by W.D. Goldbeck, 1914. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white artwork depicting a white family outside of a house in the countryside. A man sits on the porch, a woman holds a baby, an older man is playing a drum for a boy, and a girl covers her ears as two boys light fire crackers and a miniature cannon. A boy in the background watching the fireworks holds an American flag, and a small dog runs away. American Independence. Lithograph by C.H. Brainard, 1859. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Color photo depicting a woman in a white dress carrying a torch standing on a pedestal next to a man in a suit and a top hat. The man kneeling next to the woman wears a feathered headdress. The women around them wear red-and -white striped skirts and blue tops with white stars. [Fourth of July pageant at the American Colony, Jerusalem, showing women wearing red, white, and blue costumes carrying flags and a man and woman dressed as Uncle Sam and the Statue of Liberty]. Photo from the American Colony (Jerusalem), between 1905 and 1913. Prints & Photographs Division](/resource/ds.03110/)
* Two side by side black-and-white photos show a large, metal bell with a crack running up the side. The old Liberty Bell, Independence Hall, Phila., Pa. Stereograph by Keystone View Co., 1899. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows a white man wearing a black hat and a red coat holding a sword in one hand and an unrolled scroll in the other. A seal on the scroll has the year 1776 written on it. Harper's Round Table Fourth of July. Poster by Maxfield Parrish, 1895. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of a young boy holding three large firecrackers and an American flag as he smiles at the camera. He is wearing a hat and a long, white shirt with a belt. July 4th. Photo copyrighted by F. A. Loumis, 1906. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo shows men sitting in a line with their backs to the camera pulling on a large rope that another group of men facing the camera are pulling in the opposite direction. A crowd looks on with storefronts in the background. Tug-of-war, Fourth of July, on street in Skagway, Alaska. Photo by Frank G. Carpenter, 1900-1916. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of a red, one-story building with American flags mounted to the top advertising different fireworks with posters for "Black Cat Fireworks" and "Moon Traveler bottle rockets." Fireworks outlet near Decatur, Alabama. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, 2010. Prints & Photographs Division

## Free to Use and Reuse: Independence Day

American Independence Day, also known as the Fourth of July, became a federal holiday in 1938. The pictures highlight events and people from the American Revolution, the Declaration of Independence, lively celebrations, and red-white-and-blue symbols.

Browse more content that is free to use and reuse

* Color photo of red and white fireworks erupting in the sky over the Lincoln Memorial, the Washington Monument, and the U.S. Capitol Building in the background. The fireworks are reflecting on the river in the foreground, and there are boats on the water. July 4th fireworks, Washington, D.C. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, 2008. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of Black people, primarily women, gathering near a rural store. An American flag hangs from a line running between the two-story building and a large tree. A Fourth of July celebration, St. Helena Island, S.C. Photo by Marion Post Wolcott, 1939. Prints & Photographs Division

Prints & Photographs Division

* Color photo of a dark sky and a city skyline with golden fireworks erupting from the tops of skyscrapers. Fireworks over Houston, Texas. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, between 1980 and 2006. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white portrait photo of a group of adolescent white girls wearing identical dresses with striped skirts and stars around the waist. Their tall hats have bows on the top. They look at the camera but only a few are smiling. Takoma Park, July 4th celebration. Photo by the National Photo Co, 1922. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of people of all ages swimming in a body of water with a diving board built on a platform that has an American flag. Bridgeton, New Jersey. Fourth of July picnic. Photo by John Collier, Jr., 1942. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork depicting a Black man and a white man, two women, and a girl raising the American flag on a tall flagpole. In the background, the sun is rising behind a large building with a dome. The flag that has waved one hundred years--A scene on the morning of the fourth day of July 1876. Dominique C. Fabronius, artist. Chromolithograph by National Chromo Co., 1876. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of a white boy looking at boxes of cap pistols through a shop window. Fireworks for sale, Fourth of July, Vale, Oregon. Photo by Russell Lee, 1941. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork of a young white woman wearing a bathing costume made of a red and white striped skirt and a blue top with white stars. She is wearing a red bathing cap and standing on a beach shore. Follow the flag. Cover for _Puck_ by W.D. Goldbeck, 1914. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white artwork depicting a white family outside of a house in the countryside. A man sits on the porch, a woman holds a baby, an older man is playing a drum for a boy, and a girl covers her ears as two boys light fire crackers and a miniature cannon. A boy in the background watching the fireworks holds an American flag, and a small dog runs away. American Independence. Lithograph by C.H. Brainard, 1859. Prints & Photographs Division
* [Color photo depicting a woman in a white dress carrying a torch standing on a pedestal next to a man in a suit and a top hat. The man kneeling next to the woman wears a feathered headdress. The women around them wear red-and -white striped skirts and blue tops with white stars. [Fourth of July pageant at the American Colony, Jerusalem, showing women wearing red, white, and blue costumes carrying flags and a man and woman dressed as Uncle Sam and the Statue of Liberty]. Photo from the American Colony (Jerusalem), between 1905 and 1913. Prints & Photographs Division](/resource/ds.03110/)
* Two side by side black-and-white photos show a large, metal bell with a crack running up the side. The old Liberty Bell, Independence Hall, Phila., Pa. Stereograph by Keystone View Co., 1899. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color artwork shows a white man wearing a black hat and a red coat holding a sword in one hand and an unrolled scroll in the other. A seal on the scroll has the year 1776 written on it. Harper's Round Table Fourth of July. Poster by Maxfield Parrish, 1895. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo of a young boy holding three large firecrackers and an American flag as he smiles at the camera. He is wearing a hat and a long, white shirt with a belt. July 4th. Photo copyrighted by F. A. Loumis, 1906. Prints & Photographs Division
* Black-and-white photo shows men sitting in a line with their backs to the camera pulling on a large rope that another group of men facing the camera are pulling in the opposite direction. A crowd looks on with storefronts in the background. Tug-of-war, Fourth of July, on street in Skagway, Alaska. Photo by Frank G. Carpenter, 1900-1916. Prints & Photographs Division
* Color photo of a red, one-story building with American flags mounted to the top advertising different fireworks with posters for "Black Cat Fireworks" and "Moon Traveler bottle rockets." Fireworks outlet near Decatur, Alabama. Photo by Carol M. Highsmith, 2010. Prints & Photographs Division

Show More Images

"The Ladies' Book of Etiquette, and Manual of Politeness" by Florence Hartley is a guidebook on social conduct written in the mid-19th century. This comprehensive manual serves to instruct women on the principles of good manners and proper behavior in various social situations, as well as the expectations placed on them in polite society. The book aims to equip readers with the knowledge to navigate social interactions gracefully and respectfully. The opening portion of the book establishes a foundational philosophy of politeness, emphasizing that true courtesy arises from genuine kindness and an unselfish regard for the feelings of others. Hartley discusses various aspects of politeness, including the importance of considering others' feelings, maintaining a pleasant demeanor, and the idea that politeness is the outward expression of inner goodness. The introduction sets the tone for the book as it introduces the guiding principle, "Do unto others as you would have others do unto you," and establishes etiquette as an essential element in every social interaction, from casual conversations to formal gatherings. "Routledge's Manual of Etiquette" by George Routledge is a detailed guide on social manners and conduct written during the late 19th century. The book covers a wide range of topics relevant to social interactions, including etiquette for introductions, courtship, dining, and dancing. It aims to instruct both ladies and gentlemen on the expectations of respectable society, highlighting the importance of good breeding and manners. The opening of the manual outlines key concepts in social etiquette, particularly focusing on the protocols surrounding introductions. It emphasizes that the act of introducing two people is a significant responsibility that requires careful consideration of their compatibility and willingness. The text details proper forms of greeting and interaction, such as the gentleman always being introduced to the lady and not offering one's hand in greeting. Additionally, it provides specific guidelines on how to manage social situations to maintain good taste and manners, with an emphasis on the importance of decorum and respect in all interactions. ## Cigar Pairings

Whether celebrating a milestone or simply savoring the moment, a cigar is defined by the smoking experience. When choosing an accompanying beverage, light bodied cigars often pair best with refreshing white wines or light spirits, while red wines and darker spirits help bring out the flavors in full-bodied cigars. No matter your preference, a superior smoke should be paired with something of comparable quality to sip.

THE DOMINICAN ESSENTIAL:

## Rum

Both crafted in the tropics, cigars and rum are a quintessential, kindred pairing. The sweet, spicy, and floral notes of a well-aged rum effortlessly complement and balance a range of premium cigars.

* * *

THE CLASSIC:

## COFFEE

Coffee and cigars are both deeply personal rituals that, when brought together, can intensify the experience of each. Like all pairings, selecting flavors that work together, rather than overpower one another, is an art. Strong black coffee will pair perfectly with something full-bodied, like our La Aurora 100 Años cigar, but if you prefer something sweet and smooth, enjoy a cappuccino with a León Jimenes Connecticut cigar.

* * *

THE SIGNATURE:

## SPIRITS OF CHOICE

When choosing a spirit to enjoy with your cigar, there are selections, such as cognac, that stand the test of time, while an array of modern cocktails serve up unexpected enjoyment. Both traditionally enjoyed after dinner, cognac and cigars are a natural pairing for winding down an evening. Sweet, smoky cocktails, such as the Old Fashioned, easily balance even the most full-bodied cigars, while strong, boozy elixirs, like a Martini or Gin & Tonic, easily hold their own when paired with your selected cigar.

* Preferidos 1903 Emerald Tubo Cigar

  La Aurora
  
  ### Preferidos 1903 Emerald Tubo
  
  (0)

  Unit price / per
  
  FRUIT & SPICE,Medium-full Strength _1 shapes_

* * *

THE INDULGENT:

## WINE

While cigars are more traditionally enjoyed with strong, straight spirits, the right wine can deliver a smooth, satisfying experience that’s unique and unexpected. If you prefer white wines, chardonnay is a natural choice thanks to its aged oaky flavor—enjoy a glass with our La Aurora 107 Ecuador cigar for a rich, refreshing indulgence. A variety of red wines are perfectly suited cigar pairings, especially full-bodied selections from Italy and Spain which serve as rich, robust accompaniments to your cigar of choice. For celebrations and special occasions that deserve extra opulence, there is no pairing more luxurious than cigars and champagne.
